highlight.title: Major BGP Incidents Are Teaching Us About Internet Fragility
highlight.summary: Recent route leaks and BGP hijacks show how misconfigurations can knock out massive chunks of the internet in minutes. These incidents highlight both the elegance and fragility of the routing system that keeps our connected world running.

resources.title: How DNS Works: Simple Guide for Beginners (2026 Update)
resources.why_now: Perfect starting point that explains DNS like calling friends by name instead of phone numbers. Makes the internet's phone book accessible to anyone.

resources.title: What Happens When You Type a URL Into Your Browser
resources.why_now: Step-by-step breakdown of the entire journey from typing to page load. Shows how DNS, TCP handshakes, and HTTP work together in real-world scenarios.

resources.title: 8 Hot Networking Trends for 2026
resources.why_now: AI is reshaping networks with Wi-Fi 7 rollouts and automated operations gaining momentum. Essential for understanding where enterprise networking is heading.

resources.title: A Brief History of the Internet's Biggest BGP Incidents
resources.why_now: Technical deep-dive into routing failures that brought down YouTube, hijacked crypto funds, and caused global outages. Shows the critical infrastructure most people never think about.

claude_take: <cite index="1-3,2-1,24-1,28-1">2026 is proving to be a pivotal year for understanding network resilience, with multiple high-profile BGP incidents and AI traffic driving unprecedented infrastructure demands.</cite> <cite index="12-19,18-6">What's fascinating is how DNS—truly the internet's invisible workhorse—remains both elegantly simple and critically important, even as everything else gets more complex.</cite> <cite index="31-15,32-13">The next time a webpage loads instantly, remember that an entire orchestra of protocols performed in perfect harmony, usually in under a second.</cite>
